name: Italy (naval ensign)

colors.symbolism: background of the Amalfi quarter and Savoy crowning
the gold of the rostral crown

description: Italian green-white-red tricolour with the coat of arms of the Italian Navy (a quartered shield bearing the symbols of the four historic maritime republics, surmounted by a rostral crown) centred on the white stripe.
history: The current naval ensign was adopted on 9 November 1947, with the rostral crown distinguishing it from the merchant marine ensign (which uses a mural crown).

features.role: Coat of arms of the Italian Navy
features.symbolism: the four arms of the historic maritime republics: the red cross of Genoa (canton), the white cross of Pisa (chief), the gold cross on blue of Amalfi (3rd) and the lion of Saint Mark of Venice (4th), surmounted by a rostral crown
